title: 1/f Noise: The Ubiquitous Low-Frequency Noise
description: Master the physics of 1/f (pink) noise, the dominant low-frequency noise source in solid-state quantum devices. Learn about its origins from two-level systems, measurement techniques, and its profound impact on qubit dephasing.
learningObjectives: Explain the physical origins of 1/f noise in superconducting circuits
Derive the relationship between 1/f noise and Gaussian dephasing
Calculate qubit dephasing rates from 1/f noise amplitude
Apply dynamical decoupling to mitigate 1/f noise effects
Characterize 1/f noise using Ramsey and echo experiments
